binaries.title: ntfs.sys - Negative-value input validation in three NtSetInformationFile handlers (NtfsSet{Position,Allocation,EndOfFile}Info) promoted from Velocity-feature-gated (g_Feature_2614832441 / EvaluateCurrentState) to unconditional; delivered defense-in-depth hardening, no demonstrated corruption/EoP

changed_functions.name: NtfsSetPositionInfo
key_changes: Unpatched (@0x1C01D2D10): `v2 = *(a2+0x18); if (EvaluateCurrentState() && *v2 < 0) reject; else { *(a1+0x68) = *v2; return 0; }` — the non-negative check on the new CurrentByteOffset runs only when the Velocity gate EvaluateCurrentState() returns true.
Patched (@0x1C01D2CE0): EvaluateCurrentState() removed; validation is unconditional `if (*v2 >= 0) { store; return 0; } else return 0xC000000D`.
Effect: a negative CurrentByteOffset could be written to FileObject+0x68 in the pre-patch build only when the feature was explicitly Disabled (gate false); it is now always rejected.
changed_functions.security_notes: a2+0x18 -> user FILE_POSITION_INFORMATION; *v2 is the attacker-controlled 64-bit CurrentByteOffset. Pre-patch the negative-value rejection was active by default (gate returns cachedstate!=1) and bypassed only if g_Feature_2614832441 was Disabled. Any impact requires a later position-relative ReadFile/WriteFile to consume the stored offset; no such impact is demonstrated in the binary. Defense-in-depth input validation.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
; UNPATCHED NtfsSetPositionInfo @ 0x1C01D2D10 (verbatim from .asm)
mov     [rsp+arg_0], rbx
push    rdi
sub     rsp, 20h
mov     rbx, [rdx+18h]            ; user FILE_POSITION_INFORMATION
mov     rdi, rcx
call    EvaluateCurrentState      ; 0x1C00303D0 Velocity gate
test    eax, eax
jz      loc_1C01D2D52             ; gate FALSE -> store (skip sign check)
cmp     qword ptr [rbx], 0        ; sign check only when gate TRUE
jge     loc_1C01D2D52             ; >=0 -> store
mov     cl, cs:NtfsStatusDebugFlags
mov     ebx, 0C000000Dh
... reject (trace 0xF366A) ...
loc_1C01D2D52:
mov     rax, [rbx]
mov     [rdi+68h], rax            ; store CurrentByteOffset (shared target of jz AND jge)
xor     eax, eax
; PATCHED @ 0x1C01D2CE0: EvaluateCurrentState removed; `mov rdx,[rax]; test rdx,rdx; jns store` unconditional (reject trace 0xF3668). NOTE: the earlier report mis-ordered this listing (store shown on the jz fall-through); the store is at the shared loc_1C01D2D52 target.

changed_functions.name: NtfsSetAllocationInfo
key_changes: Unpatched (@0x1C00DFA10): `v14 = EvaluateCurrentState(); if (v14) { if (v13 > *(Vcb+0x1E18) || v13 < 0) reject; } else if (v13 > *(Vcb+0x1E18)) reject;` — in the gate-false branch only the upper bound (max file size) is checked; the `v13 < 0` test is omitted.
Patched (@0x1C00DFA10): `if (v14 > *(*(a4+0xB0)+0x1E18) || v14 < 0) reject;` — single unconditional check applying both the max-size and the negative test; EvaluateCurrentState() removed.
changed_functions.security_notes: When the feature was Disabled (gate false), a negative AllocationSize passed (negative is not > positive max, and the <0 test was skipped) and routed to the shrink path NtfsPrepareToShrinkFileSize(a1, Scb, a4, v13). The negative-value rejection was on by default; the patch makes it unconditional and retires the feature. No downstream out-of-bounds/pool-corrupting write is demonstrated in the disassembly; impact stands as a negative size entering NTFS allocation bookkeeping under a non-default configuration.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
; UNPATCHED NtfsSetAllocationInfo @ 0x1C00DFA10 (guard region):
;   v13 = **(a3+0x18);                         // user AllocationSize
;   v14 = EvaluateCurrentState();              // call 0x1C00303D0 Velocity gate
;   v16 = *(a4+0xB0);                          // Vcb
;   if (v14) { if (v13 > *(v16+0x1E18) || v13 < 0) reject; }   // full check (trace 0xF379F)
;   else if (v13 > *(v16+0x1E18)) reject;      // gate FALSE: upper bound only (trace 0xF37A6)
; PATCHED @ 0x1C00DFA10: EvaluateCurrentState removed; single guard `v14 > *(*(a4+0xB0)+0x1E18) || v14 < 0` -> reject (trace 0xF379A).

changed_functions.name: NtfsSetEndOfFileInfo
key_changes: Unpatched (@0x1C011B620): `v12 = **(a3+0x18); if (EvaluateCurrentState() && v12 < 0) reject;` — negative EndOfFile rejected only when the gate returns true (the && short-circuits when gate false).
Patched (@0x1C011B600): `if (v12 < 0) reject;` — negative EndOfFile always rejected; EvaluateCurrentState() removed.
changed_functions.security_notes: When the feature was Disabled, a negative EndOfFile bypassed the sign check and entered NTFS size handling (Scb size / valid-data-length). The rejection was on by default; the patch makes it unconditional. Downstream size/valid-data-length confusion is plausible but not demonstrated in the binary. Same root cause as the other two handlers.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
; UNPATCHED NtfsSetEndOfFileInfo @ 0x1C011B620 (guard region):
;   v12 = **(a3+0x18);
;   call 0x1C00303D0 (EvaluateCurrentState); test eax,eax; je <accept>   // gate false skips sign check
;   test v12,v12; jns <accept>                                          // reached only when gate true
;   <reject>: STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER 0xC000000D (trace 0xF3AD5)
; PATCHED @ 0x1C011B600: EvaluateCurrentState removed; `test v12,v12; js <reject>` unconditional (trace 0xF3AC6).

security_findings.entry_point: NtSetInformationFile(FileHandle, ..., FileInformationClass = FileAllocationInformation (19), InputBuffer=FILE_ALLOCATION_INFORMATION{ LONGLONG AllocationSize })
call_chain: NtfsPrepareToShrinkFileSize with negative size (no demonstrated corrupting write)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Missing/feature-gated input validation of a signed 64-bit value (CWE-20), now unconditional
security_findings.description: In the pre-patch build the rejection of a negative new AllocationSize was performed only when EvaluateCurrentState() returned true. That helper is a WIL/Velocity gate (EvaluateFeature(&g_Feature_2614832441_60270648...) returning cachedstate != 1), so the rejection was active by default and skipped only if the feature was explicitly Disabled. In the gate-false branch only the upper bound (AllocationSize > *(Vcb+0x1E18)) was checked; a negative 64-bit AllocationSize is not greater than the positive max, so with the omitted < 0 test it was accepted and routed to NtfsPrepareToShrinkFileSize. The patch removes the gate and always applies `size > max || size < 0`, and the g_Feature_2614832441 descriptor is retired from the image. No out-of-bounds/pool-corrupting write derived from the negative value is demonstrated in the disassembly.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Requires the g_Feature_2614832441 feature in the (non-default) Disabled state so EvaluateCurrentState() returns false. Then open a handle to a file on an NTFS volume with write/append access and issue NtSetInformationFile with FileAllocationInformation and AllocationSize = a negative LARGE_INTEGER (e.g. 0x8000000000000000). Under default settings the pre-patch build already rejects this.
security_findings.primitive: Attacker-controlled negative AllocationSize accepted into NTFS allocation bookkeeping (shrink path) under a non-default feature-disabled configuration. No memory-corruption or privilege-escalation primitive is demonstrated in the binary. Defense-in-depth input validation.
security_findings.assembly: ; UNPATCHED @ 0x1C00DFA10: call EvaluateCurrentState; test eax,eax; gate-true does cmp v13,[Vcb+0x1E18] AND the <0 test; gate-false does upper-bound compare only (reject trace 0xF37A6). PATCHED @ 0x1C00DFA10: EvaluateCurrentState removed; single guard `> max || < 0` (reject trace 0xF379A).

breakpoints.why: Entry; new AllocationSize = **(a3+0x18). Inspect the 64-bit value for the sign bit.
Velocity gate EvaluateFeature(&g_Feature_2614832441_60270648...); returns cachedstate != 1. EAX==0 (feature Disabled) selects the branch that skips the < 0 test. Note this is a non-default state.

watch: eax after call 0x1C00303D0 (EvaluateCurrentState) — 0 only when the feature is Disabled
[Vcb+0x1E18] where Vcb = *(a4+0xB0) — the max-file-size upper bound
key_offsets: unpatched gate-false reject block trace id 0xF37A6; unpatched gate-true reject 0xF379F
patched single guard reject block trace id 0xF379A
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Set g_Feature_2614832441 to Disabled (non-default), then CreateFile on an NTFS file with write access and call NtSetInformationFile(h, &iosb, &alloc, sizeof(alloc), FileAllocationInformation) with a negative AllocationSize.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: With the feature Disabled, the unpatched call returns STATUS_SUCCESS (0) for a negative AllocationSize; the patched call (and the default-configured unpatched call) returns STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0xC000000D). No corrupting write is demonstrated.
debugging_aids.notes: arg4 = Scb (Vcb at Scb+0xB0, max size at Vcb+0x1E18). The three handlers shared EvaluateCurrentState @ 0x1C00303D0; it is removed in the patched image.

security_findings.entry_point: NtSetInformationFile(FileHandle, ..., FileInformationClass = FileEndOfFileInformation (20), InputBuffer=FILE_END_OF_FILE_INFORMATION{ LONGLONG EndOfFile })
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Missing/feature-gated input validation of a signed 64-bit value (CWE-20), now unconditional
security_findings.description: Pre-patch, `if (EvaluateCurrentState() && v12 < 0) reject;` — the negative EndOfFile (new FileSize) was rejected only when the Velocity gate returned true (active by default; skipped only when the feature was Disabled). When the gate was false the negative FileSize was accepted into NTFS size handling. The patch changes this to the unconditional `if (v12 < 0) reject;` and retires the feature.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: g_Feature_2614832441 in the non-default Disabled state. Handle to an NTFS file with write access; NtSetInformationFile with FileEndOfFileInformation and EndOfFile = a negative LARGE_INTEGER.
security_findings.primitive: Attacker-controlled negative EndOfFile/FileSize accepted into NTFS size state under a non-default feature-disabled configuration. Downstream size/valid-data-length confusion is plausible but not demonstrated; no OOB/EoP primitive is shown. Defense-in-depth input validation.
security_findings.assembly: ; UNPATCHED @ 0x1C011B620: call 0x1C00303D0 (EvaluateCurrentState); test eax,eax; je <accept> (gate false skips sign check); test v12,v12; jns <accept>; <reject> trace 0xF3AD5. PATCHED @ 0x1C011B600: EvaluateCurrentState removed; `test v12,v12; js <reject>` unconditional (trace 0xF3AC6).

breakpoints.why: Entry; v12 = **(a3+0x18) holds the attacker-controlled new EndOfFile. Watch whether the < 0 test is reached (skipped when EvaluateCurrentState() returns false).
Velocity gate; EAX==0 (feature Disabled, non-default) short-circuits the && and skips the negative-EndOfFile rejection.

watch: eax after call 0x1C00303D0 — 0 only when the feature is Disabled
key_offsets: `EvaluateCurrentState() && v12 < 0` = conditional sign check; patched tests v12 sign unconditionally (reject trace 0xF3AC6, was 0xF3AD5)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Set g_Feature_2614832441 Disabled; CreateFile on an NTFS file with FILE_WRITE_DATA; NtSetInformationFile(h,&iosb,&eof,sizeof(eof),FileEndOfFileInformation) with a negative EndOfFile.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: With the feature Disabled, unpatched returns STATUS_SUCCESS for a negative EndOfFile; patched (and default-configured unpatched) returns 0xC000000D. No corrupting write is demonstrated.
debugging_aids.notes: Same shared gate at 0x1C00303D0. FileInformationClass 20 = FileEndOfFileInformation.

security_findings.entry_point: NtSetInformationFile(FileHandle, ..., FileInformationClass = FilePositionInformation (14), InputBuffer=FILE_POSITION_INFORMATION{ LONGLONG CurrentByteOffset })
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Missing/feature-gated input validation of a signed 64-bit value (CWE-20), now unconditional
security_findings.description: Pre-patch, `v2 = *(a2+0x18); if (EvaluateCurrentState() && *v2 < 0) reject; else { *(a1+0x68) = *v2; return 0; }` — when the Velocity gate returned false (feature Disabled, non-default) the non-negative check was short-circuited and a negative CurrentByteOffset was stored at FileObject+0x68. The patch removes EvaluateCurrentState() and makes the >= 0 check unconditional.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: g_Feature_2614832441 in the non-default Disabled state. Any handle to an NTFS file; NtSetInformationFile with FilePositionInformation and CurrentByteOffset = a negative LARGE_INTEGER.
security_findings.primitive: Negative FileObject->CurrentByteOffset stored under a non-default feature-disabled configuration. Any effect requires a subsequent position-relative ReadFile/WriteFile to consume the offset; not demonstrated in the binary. Lowest-impact of the three; defense-in-depth input validation.
security_findings.assembly: ; UNPATCHED @ 0x1C01D2D10 (verbatim): call EvaluateCurrentState; test eax,eax; jz loc_1C01D2D52; cmp qword ptr [rbx],0; jge loc_1C01D2D52; <reject trace 0xF366A>; loc_1C01D2D52: mov rax,[rbx]; mov [rdi+68h],rax; xor eax,eax. The store is the shared target of BOTH jz and jge. PATCHED @ 0x1C01D2CE0: mov rdx,[rax]; test rdx,rdx; jns store (unconditional), else reject (trace 0xF3668). NOTE: the store must be shown at loc_1C01D2D52, not on the jz fall-through.

breakpoints.why: Entry of the FilePositionInformation handler. rdx+0x18 -> user FILE_POSITION_INFORMATION; qword[rbx] is the attacker CurrentByteOffset; rcx (arg1)+0x68 is the store target.
Velocity gate; when it returns 0 (feature Disabled, non-default) the jz jumps to the store, skipping cmp qword[rbx],0 / jge.

watch: rbx = pointer to user CurrentByteOffset; qword[rbx] is the signed value
eax after call 0x1C00303D0 — 0 only when the feature is Disabled
key_offsets: jz loc_1C01D2D52 after test eax,eax = gate-false path to the store (skips the sign check)
cmp qword [rbx],0 / jge loc_1C01D2D52 = the sign check, reached only when gate true
loc_1C01D2D52: mov [rdi+68h], rax = the store (shared target of jz AND jge)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Set g_Feature_2614832441 Disabled; CreateFile on an NTFS file; NtSetInformationFile(h,&iosb,&pos,sizeof(pos),FilePositionInformation) with a negative CurrentByteOffset, then a position-relative ReadFile/WriteFile.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: With the feature Disabled, unpatched stores the negative value into FileObject+0x68 and returns STATUS_SUCCESS; patched (and default-configured unpatched) returns 0xC000000D. No fault/OOB is demonstrated.
debugging_aids.notes: FileInformationClass 14 = FilePositionInformation. The earlier report's verbatim asm mis-ordered the store; see key_offsets.

summary: The patch to ntfs.sys touches exactly three NtSetInformationFile handlers - NtfsSetPositionInfo (FilePositionInformation, class 14), NtfsSetAllocationInfo (FileAllocationInformation, class 19), and NtfsSetEndOfFileInfo (FileEndOfFileInformation, class 20) - and in each it removes a Windows Velocity feature gate (EvaluateCurrentState @ 0x1C00303D0, which is EvaluateFeature(&g_Feature_2614832441_60270648...) returning cachedstate != 1) that made the rejection of an attacker-supplied negative 64-bit value conditional. The rejection is now unconditional, and both the EvaluateCurrentState helper and the g_Feature_2614832441 descriptor are fully retired from the patched image (a delivered fix / retired kill-switch, not staging retained). Importantly, the gate returns true (validation active, negatives rejected) unless the feature is explicitly Disabled, so the accept-negatives behavior existed only under a non-default configuration that an unprivileged local caller does not control. The proven primitive is attacker-controlled negative CurrentByteOffset / AllocationSize / EndOfFile entering NTFS position/size/allocation bookkeeping under that non-default state; no out-of-bounds access, pool corruption, information disclosure, or privilege escalation is demonstrated in the disassembly. The original report's HIGH/HIGH/MEDIUM ratings and its pool-corruption -> LPE exploit chain (with KASLR/pool-spray/token-steal/SMEP/HVCI/CFG narrative) are unsubstantiated and have been removed; corrected severity is Low (defense-in-depth input validation) with classification over-claimed-but-real.
